parentQuestion: A toxin prevents the assembly of microtubules in dividing cells. The most immedi...

scaffolds.q: The structure that physically separates chromosomes during cell division is the ___.
scaffolds.explain: The mitotic spindle — made of microtubules — attaches to chromosomes and pulls them to opposite poles of the dividing cell. It's the cellular tow truck, darling. Keep this momentum:.

scaffolds.q: Microtubules are protein filaments made of subunits called ___.
scaffolds.explain: Tubulin dimers (α and β tubulin) polymerize to form hollow microtubule tubes. Actin forms microfilaments, not microtubules. Different filament, different protein, darling.
